snippet.title: When Does a Bold Promise Become a Crime? The Rise and Fall of Theranos
snippet.description:
Theranos promised a blood-testing revolution. The legal question is narrower and harder: when does a bold business promise become criminal fraud?

This episode follows the record: the investor-fraud counts that produced convictions, the patient counts that resulted in acquittals, and the counts with no verdict. Acquittal is not treated as exoneration; the distinction is the point.

Visual note: this video uses licensed stock/factory media, AI-generated symbolic reconstructions, and motion design. No real-person likeness of Elizabeth Holmes, Ramesh Balwani, or any other real person is intended.
